title: 9 Herbal Creams For Difficulty Chewing
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a game-changer for people who struggle with difficulty chewing. This can be due to various reasons such as toothaches, gum inflammation, or even dental work. Herbal teas, particularly those made from certain plants, can help alleviate this issue by reducing inflammation, soothing the mouth, and promoting healing.  One example is Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease gum inflammation and toothaches. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has natural pain-relieving qualities that can help reduce discomfort while eating. Eucalyptus globulus, known for its decongestant properties, can help clear out any blockages in the sinuses that might be contributing to difficulty chewing.  By using these herbal teas as a mouthwash or applying a cream infused with these herbs, you can experience several benefits. For one, you'll be able to eat and drink with more comfort, which can improve your overall nutrition and energy levels. Additionally, the soothing and anti-inflammatory effects can help prevent further damage to your mouth and teeth. By incorporating herbal creams into your daily routine, you can take a step towards reclaiming control over your eating habits and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains a number of bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and triterpenoids. These compounds work together to reduce inflammation and irritation in the mouth and throat, making it easier to chew and swallow. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe sore mouth tissues and relax tense muscles. Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant properties that can help to protect the mouth and throat from damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help to alleviate difficulty chewing and make eating easier.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and mix well until combined.
Remove from heat and add shea butter and vitamin E oil. Mix until smooth and creamy.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden. Once hardened, use as a cream to help with difficulty chewing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains a compound called gingerol, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory. This active constituent helps to reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to chew food. Another key compound in ginger is shogaol, a natural analgesic that blocks pain signals to the brain, reducing discomfort. The warm, soothing properties of ginger also help to relax the muscles in the face and jaw, allowing for easier movement and better chewing. By reducing inflammation, pain, and muscle tension, Zingiber officinale creams can help alleviate difficulties with chewing.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder, 2 tablespoons of ginger powder (Zingiber officinale), and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the turmeric and ginger powders to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches 120-130°F (49-54°C).
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify completely. Once solid, add a few drops of vitamin E oil on top and use as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains bioactive compounds like eucalyptol, caffeic acid, and diosphenol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe irritated tissues in the mouth and throat. The anti-inflammatory properties of eucalyptol and caffeic acid reduce swelling and pain, making it easier to chew and swallow. Diosphenol, a compound found in eucalyptus, has a numbing effect that can help relax tense muscles in the jaw and mouth. By reducing inflammation, pain, and muscle tension, eucalyptus globulus creams can make it easier to chew and manage difficulty chewing.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a saucepan.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter in the saucepan over low heat, stirring until smooth.
Add 1 tablespoon of eucalyptus oil to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and slowly pour in 1 cup of cooled distilled water, stirring constantly.
Pour the mixture into a bowl and let it cool and solidify before transferring it to a jar for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains bioactive constituents such as alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to chew. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Echinacea purpurea creams also have antioxidant properties, which help protect the mucous membranes in the mouth from damage and promote healing. The anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea purpurea creams may also help reduce stiffness and discomfort in the jaw and facial muscles, making it easier to chew and swallow. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Echinacea purpurea creams can help alleviate difficulty chewing caused by conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a bowl.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 30 seconds.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the coconut oil and let it steep for 4 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another bowl. Discard the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter with the Echinacea purpurea infusion. Pour into a jar and let it cool and solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains a high concentration of menthol, menthone, and limonene. These bioactive constituents have a soothing effect on the muscles and mucous membranes in the mouth, which can help ease discomfort and stiffness that makes chewing difficult. Menthol's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and pain, while limonene's antioxidant properties help protect the mouth from damage and irritation. Menthone's antispasmodic properties also help relax the muscles, allowing for easier movement and function of the jaw and mouth. By reducing pain, inflammation, and muscle tension, Mentha x piperita creams can help improve chewing and swallowing difficulties.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1 tablespoon of Mentha x piperita essential oil, and 1/4 cup of shea butter.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl in the microwave (30 seconds at a time, until melted).
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until combined.
Add Mentha x piperita essential oil to the mixture and stir well. The final mixture should be smooth and creamy.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden. Once hardened, your Mentha x piperita cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the mouth and jaw. Curcumin also has antioxidant properties that protect the gums and mucous membranes from damage caused by free radicals. Turmeric, the plant from which Curcuma longa is derived, contains a compound called gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help ease discomfort and pain associated with difficulty chewing. Curcuma longa creams also contain a compound called boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and pain in the mouth and jaw. These properties help make Curcuma longa creams a good option for people who experience difficulty chewing due to pain and inflammation in the mouth and jaw.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (from Curcuma longa), 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel.
Melt the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the beeswax to the melted coconut oil and stir until melted.
Add the turmeric powder, vitamin E oil, and aloe vera gel to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken. Pour into containers and use as needed to help with difficulty chewing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains bioactive constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, which help with difficulty chewing. Aloin and aloe-emodin have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the mouth and throat, making it easier to chew and swallow. Acemannan, a polysaccharide, stimulates the immune system and promotes wound healing, which is beneficial for mouth sores and ulcers that can cause difficulty chewing. The creams' soothing and moisturizing properties also calm irritated tissues and reduce discomfort, allowing for more comfortable eating and swallowing. The anti-inflammatory and immune-stimulating properties of the bioactive constituents in Aloe barbadensis creams make them a helpful treatment for people experiencing difficulty chewing.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
In a small saucepan, heat the distilled water over low heat. Add the beeswax and stir until melted.
Remove the saucepan from heat and add the coconut oil, shea butter, and aloe vera gel. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar or container. Let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once solid, your Aloe barbadensis cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected area as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains sesquiterpene lactones, triterpene acids, and carotenoids, which are key active constituents that make it effective for difficulty chewing. The sesquiterpene lactones, particularly calendulic acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw. The triterpene acids, such as oleanolic acid, help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, making it easier to chew and swallow. The carotenoids, including beta-carotene, have antioxidant properties that protect the mouth and jaw from further irritation and damage. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Calendula officinalis creams can help to alleviate difficulty chewing and promote oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Infuse the Calendula flowers in coconut oil by mixing them in a clean glass jar. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl. Discard the Calendula flowers.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Combine the melted mixture with the infused oil and vitamin E oil. Stir well and pour into small containers. Let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains essential oils with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The main bioactive constituents are carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid, and camphor, which help reduce muscle tension and inflammation. These properties can help relax the muscles involved in chewing, making it easier to eat and digest food. The anti-inflammatory effect of carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid may also help soothe sore throats and mouth ulcers that can make chewing painful. By reducing muscle tension and inflammation, Rosmarinus officinalis creams can provide relief for people with difficulty chewing.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for difficulty chewing
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 10 drops of Rosemary essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in the Rosemary essential oil. Let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool completely. Use as needed to soothe difficulty chewing.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent difficulty chewing from forming. Some teas, like ginger and peppermint, contain anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe sore jaws and ease muscle tension. Regular consumption may also improve digestion, which can help reduce discomfort while eating and promote healthy eating habits.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can help with difficulty chewing, but it's essential to be cautious. Some teas, like slippery elm and marshmallow root, might cause allergic reactions or interact with medications. Drinking them daily can also lead to stomach issues if not consumed in moderation. It's best to start with small amounts and observe how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with difficulty chewing, but the time it takes to see results varies. Some people notice improvement within a few days to a week, while others may take a few weeks. It depends on the specific herbal tea and the individual's condition.
faq_time: For difficulty chewing, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning. This is because the digestive system is most active during breakfast, helping to break down food and soothe sore muscles in the mouth and throat. A warm cup of herbal tea can provide gentle relief as you start your day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for difficulty chewing. For example, certain teas like ginkgo biloba might increase the risk of bleeding if you're taking blood thinners. St. John's Wort can also interact with medications, changing how they work in your body. It's essential to be aware of these possible interactions.
faq_pregnant: During pregnancy, some herbal teas may not be safe to drink. If you have trouble chewing, your doctor might recommend a soft diet instead of herbal tea. Some herbs can affect your baby's growth or cause problems during delivery. It's best to choose plain, caffeine-free water to stay hydrated.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a helpful way to make swallowing easier when you're having trouble chewing while breastfeeding. Many herbs, like slippery elm and marshmallow root, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe mouth and throat irritation, making it more comfortable to drink liquids and eat soft foods.
title_2: 9 Herbal Creams to Ease Chewing Struggles for Denture Users
